content:
Greetings,

I've been playing around with Rust in the kernel for a few days now and I have a question:

How am I supposed to load kernel modules? Is this supported yet? I've gone trough rust/module_param.rs but don't really have an idea on how to use it.

I've used the rust-out-of-tree repo as a guide so far.

I am new to Rust, and I really liked the idea of being able to write kernel modules in a language that isn't C.

Thanks in advance!

Marko said:

How am I supposed to load kernel modules?

Are you trying to just load a Rust kernel module? Or are you trying to use module params?

Whoops, I meant load kernel module params within my Rust module.

I have successfully compiled and loaded the rust out of tree example.
Essentially I am looking for a Rust reference for C's declaration of module params module_param fn.

The way to load a kernel module param is the same for C module params:

sudo modprobe <module_name> <parameter_name>=<value>

Alice Ryhl said:

there is some sample code in samples/rust/rust_minimal.rs

Interesting, thanks! I believe this is what I was looking for.
